feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Real life Chun Li
entry.content: Real life Chun Li
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Hello. I post here sometimes about me being a 165cm manlet. It's just that thoug…
entry.content: Hello. I post here sometimes about me being a 165cm manlet. It's just that thoughts about it suddenly fill my head, and I can't get rid of them for days, so I'm trying to talk about it here to hear from anons who have similar problem or just to vent. I try to cope with vidya but even that doesnt help sometimes. That's hard to be so short...
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >/v/ constantly complains about multiplayer being dry now >says no one is on mic…
entry.content: >/v/ constantly complains about multiplayer being dry now >says no one is on mic, making friends, or even just text chatting >meanwhile, see popular indie slop is having a free weekend >give it a try >tons of lobbies filled with people on mics, chatting, having fun, shooting the shit, bantering around >even some of the precious racial slurs and such that /v/ puts on a pedestal Could it be. Perhaps video games online are still fun, and that instead the problem is that /v/ is filled with A) boomer
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Over the years whenever someone expressed an interest in giving Linux a shot, I'…
entry.content: Over the years whenever someone expressed an interest in giving Linux a shot, I'd say >Fuck it, just install Ubuntu But about a year ago I gave Arch and Fedora a shot (on my home PC and laptop respectively) and I was shocked by how everything just works. As it turns out, all the problems I dealt with aren't some kind of "Linux tax" I have to deal with, it's just Ubuntu being ass. But in that case, what do I recommend newbies who want to dip their toes into Linux? Arch has been smooth sailing for
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Here's my desktop browser tier list.
entry.content: Not included: Ungoogled in S tier, Sliepnir in A tier. Ungoogled Chromium is one of the few browsers that are actually private but doesn't cripple your life in the process. Sliepnir is beautiful and simple. Unfortunately it doesn't work on Linux. Falkon could take it's place if KDE devs actually gave a shit. Thoughts; Pale Moon and Basilisk are awesome because they're both using the independent UXP engine. Might aswell be the same browser, it just depends on which era of Firefox you like more. F
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /slop/ - AI Slop and Image Generation Thread #3034
entry.content: >Gremlins Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. Previous Thread: https://desuarchive.org/trash/threa d/84192431/ >Scripts to check image metadata: rentry.or

entry.title: /g/ Technology: If 1m context length is supposed to be 3000 pages of text literally more than tw…
entry.content: If 1m context length is supposed to be 3000 pages of text literally more than twice the bible then wtf is going on behind the scenes with Anthropic's models for them to constantly have to compact sessions as the 1m context length is reached? I asked Fable 1 thing (in chat mode, where you're able to see all the thinking between the lines) and after 30 minutes or so it had to compact the conversation, I gave it 1 prompt, and I'm pretty sure it did not type several fucking novels
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /int/ International: Pelé played Plumberball "That last brazilian goal in 1970 "wouldn't have happene…
entry.content: Pelé played Plumberball "That last brazilian goal in 1970 "wouldn't have happened today," explains Professor Orlando Laitano of the University of Florida in the United States. He is one of the leading experts in exercise physiology. If that Brazilian team could travel through time, their play would probably be intercepted by modern opponents. And "the biggest imbalance wouldn't be talent, but physiology," according to Laitano."
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.content: I hate full fledged auto battle modes in grindy games because it means that those games are now designed around having to use it and turning your pc/console into a pseudo bitcoin miner that runs overnight if grinds are intended to be afk farmed then there should just be a way to instantly clear the fight or multiply the loot you get per fight by either using a limited resource or making the fight exponentially harder earlier disgaea games are a perfect example of grindy games that actually respe

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I've always been a big fan of ublock, but today I disabled it to test something,…
entry.content: I've always been a big fan of ublock, but today I disabled it to test something, and holy shit, Firefox is so much faster without it. I feel very conflicted right now and I'm not sure what to do anymore. It's so useful, even if just for manually blocking things, but I never realized how sluggish it makes the browser.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: What killed Lulz.net?
entry.content: What happened to Lulz.net? Was surprised recently to find it appears to be permanently offline with no IRC, Discord, or Twitter presence either. Just a 20 year old website that just got unplugged one day, for seemingly no reason?
entry.dc:creator: /trash/ Off-Topic

entry.title: /o/ Auto: need a second commuterslop car
entry.content: So I bought a C6 partially because of /o/'s recommendation. It's great, and I love driving it. However, I just got a job that's 50ish miles from my house and I have to commute every day. I don't think spending $20 on gas and driving 100 miles on my Corvette is very practical, so I'm looking at getting a beater that gets decent gas mileage, and that I won't feel bad driving into the ground. Wondering if anyone on the board has recommendations. My budget is around $3,000, I'd be willing to go up t
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I went to jail for two days. The worst part was that it was so fucking boring, a…

All of the food sucked, except for this one meal where they had this hot dog with chili sauce on it.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Ocarina of Time Remake
entry.content: So I know some if not most of you fags are gonna hate it. Hell most of you already hate it. But for those who don’t, what would you like to see out of it? Personally I would like: >A much larger Hyrule field with shit actually in it. >Castle town should also be expanded with more to do. >More enemy variety. >More dungeons. >Decent camera. >Navi settings to make it stop handholding you. >horseback combat felt added it at the last second, so definitely more with it. Anything else you would like to
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: OK, tried my best and I've run into enough walls that I have to ask. Playing La …
entry.content: OK, tried my best and I've run into enough walls that I have to ask. Playing La Mulana for the first time, getting frustrated often but overall fun game. However, there is an issue with this room. I know this is where I activate the area boss fight, I saw the ankh showing up, but decided to leave it for later because I wanted to beat the first area boss (which I had missed previously, my first boss was the mine cart chase) So I get that done, and now I want to beat the waterfall area boss, I ret
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Dr. Doom is the main character in the next Avenger's movie. Bold move, but I'm …

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: How the fuck was Palworld 1.0 this much of a success? It was a hit at launch but…
entry.content: How the fuck was Palworld 1.0 this much of a success? It was a hit at launch but the entire fanbase vacated over night because of how boring and shit it is. I legit don’t get why people are coming back, it’s worse than modern GF Pokemon by a mile and those games have questionable quality
